title: The Guide for the Perplexed
description: The Guide for the Perplexed — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "迷途指津" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Table of Contents"
memes.description: "Complete table of contents for Le Guide des Egares, Part I"

memes.label: Abraham
memes.description: 'The biblical patriarch and first monotheist — Maimonides presents Abraham as the paradigmatic philosophical seeker who discovered God through rational inquiry and whose prophetic visions illustrate key principles of the Guide.'

memes.label: Accident
memes.description: 'The Aristotelian category of accident — that which exists not in itself but in another — as employed by Maimonides in his analysis of divine attributes and critique of Kalām'

memes.label: Acquired Intellect
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' theory of the acquired intellect as the highest stage of human intellectual development, mediating between the material intellect and the Active Intellect'

memes.label: Active Intellect
memes.description: "In medieval philosophy and the Guide for the Perplexed, the Active Intellect is the lowest of the separate intelligences, actualizing human potential intellect and mediating prophetic revelation."

memes.label: Adam
memes.description: 'The first man, whose creation in the image of God and subsequent fall provide Maimonides with a paradigm for the relationship between intellect, divine command, and human perfection in the Guide.'

memes.label: "Additions et Rectifications"
memes.description: "Corrections and additions to the translation"

memes.label: Alexander of Aphrodisias
memes.description: 'The most celebrated ancient commentator on Aristotle, whose analysis of the intellect Maimonides explicitly adopts in the Guide — and whose works he recommended as essential reading.'

memes.label: Alfarabi
memes.description: 'The Second Teacher of Arabic philosophy — Maimonides'' primary source for Aristotelian logic and a key authority cited against the Mutakallimun.'

memes.label: Allegory
memes.description: 'The method of allegorical or figurative interpretation by which Maimonides explains the hidden meanings of prophetic texts and biblical narratives'

memes.label: Angels
memes.description: "In the Guide for the Perplexed, angels are identified by Maimonides with the separate intelligences of Aristotelian cosmology -- pure intellects without corporeal form."

memes.label: Anthropomorphism
memes.description: 'The error of attributing human qualities, bodily form, or physical attributes to God — which Maimonides identifies as the fundamental theological mistake, more dangerous than idolatry because it corrupts the very conception of the divine'

memes.label: Arabs
memes.description: 'The Arab-Islamic intellectual milieu in which Maimonides lived and wrote — the source of his philosophical vocabulary, scientific knowledge, and theological interlocutors.'

memes.label: Aristotelian Categories
memes.description: 'The system of ten categories or predicaments in Aristotelian logic, by which all beings are classified according to their mode of existence, as discussed by Maimonides in the Guide'

memes.label: Aristotelian Physics
memes.description: 'The Aristotelian science of nature that Maimonides identifies with the Maaseh Bereshith (Account of Creation) and prescribes as a necessary preparation for metaphysical study'

memes.label: Aristotle
memes.description: 'The Philosopher par excellence — Maimonides'' primary philosophical authority, whose framework is integrated with biblical revelation throughout the Guide.'

memes.label: Atoms
memes.description: 'The indivisible particles (atoms) posited by the Mutakallimun as the fundamental constituents of all bodies, critiqued by Maimonides in the Guide'

memes.label: Avicenna
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' "habitual guide" in Arabic Peripatetic philosophy — the originator of the essence/existence distinction and the necessary/contingent distinction adopted throughout the Guide.'

memes.label: Causality
memes.description: 'The theory of causation derived from Aristotle that Maimonides employs throughout the Guide to explain the relationship between God and the world, and which the Kalam theologians reject in favor of occasionalism'

memes.label: Celestial Spheres
memes.description: 'The concentric rotating spheres that carry the celestial bodies, whose motions and governing Intelligences are central to medieval cosmology as discussed by Maimonides in the Guide'

memes.label: Creation
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' doctrine of creation ex nihilo — God''s bringing the world into existence from nothing, as distinct from eternal emanation.'

memes.label: Creation of the World
memes.description: 'The foundational event described in Genesis — God''s creation of the world ex nihilo, interpreted allegorically by Maimonides as encoding Aristotelian physics.'

memes.label: David
memes.description: 'The second king of Israel, whose words and actions Maimonides draws upon in the Guide to illustrate principles of divine will, prophetic speech, and the reconciliation of scriptural contradictions.'

memes.label: Demonstration
memes.description: 'The Aristotelian standard of demonstrative proof (apodeixis) that Maimonides upholds as the only method capable of yielding certain knowledge of metaphysical truths'

memes.label: Divine Attributes
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' doctrine that God cannot be described by positive attributes — only via negativa or divine actions.'

memes.label: Divine Names
memes.description: 'The various names by which God is designated in Scripture — which Maimonides classifies philosophically, distinguishing the unique tetragrammaton that denotes pure existence from all other divine names that are derived from actions'

memes.label: Divine Unity
memes.description: 'The doctrine that God is absolutely one — a unity that excludes all composition, corporeality, and multiplicity of attributes — as demonstrated by Maimonides in the Guide'

memes.label: Divine Will
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' conception of the divine will as the eternal, unchanging principle governing creation and providence'

memes.label: Egypt
memes.description: 'Ancient Egypt — the nation from which Israel was delivered, referenced by Maimonides in historical and allegorical contexts.'

memes.label: Emanation
memes.description: 'The Neoplatonic doctrine of divine emanation or overflow by which existence, form, and intellect proceed from God to the lower levels of being'

memes.label: Error
memes.description: 'The cognitive failure to apprehend truth correctly — which Maimonides analyzes through a systematic taxonomy of causes, from intellectual haste and sensory limitation to the deliberate use of allegory in Scripture'

memes.label: Esoteric Interpretation
memes.description: 'The tradition of transmitting the hidden philosophical meaning of Scripture orally to qualified disciples, as distinct from exoteric or literal interpretation'

memes.label: Eternity
memes.description: 'The debate over whether the universe is eternal (Aristotelian view) or created in time with a beginning (Maimonides'' position).'

memes.label: Ethics
memes.description: 'The doctrine of moral virtues and their preparatory role for intellectual perfection, as expounded by Maimonides in the Guide for the Perplexed'

memes.label: Evil
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' theodicy — evil as privation of good, not a positive creation, linked to matter and absence of wisdom.'

memes.label: Exodus
memes.description: 'The foundational event of Israelite nationhood — the liberation from Egypt, central to Maimonides'' understanding of divine providence, lawgiving, and the nature of miracles.'

memes.label: "Fautes à Corriger"
memes.description: "Errata list for the translation"

memes.label: Final Cause
memes.description: 'The final cause or purpose — one of the four Aristotelian causes — which Maimonides affirms as applicable to God in His relation to the universe'

memes.label: Free Will
memes.description: 'The human capacity for genuine choice — a necessary condition for moral responsibility and divine commandment.'

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "Cover and title pages of Le Guide des Egares (1856)"

memes.label: God
memes.description: 'The central subject of Maimonides'' "Guide for the Perplexed" — the nature, attributes, and knowability of God as understood through reason and scripture.'

memes.label: Homonymy
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' method of identifying and interpreting equivocal Hebrew terms in the Guide'

memes.label: Human Perfection
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' doctrine of the four types of human perfection, culminating in intellectual perfection as man''s ultimate end'

memes.label: Idolatry
memes.description: 'The worship of beings other than God — stars, images, or intermediaries — which Maimonides analyses in the Guide as the historical root of nearly all Torah legislation and the fundamental error from which monotheism liberates humanity'

memes.label: Imagination
memes.description: 'The cognitive faculty that Maimonides distinguishes sharply from the intellect — capable of combining sensory forms into novel images, essential for prophecy in non-Mosaic prophets, but incapable of grasping immaterial truths and a source of error when mistaken for reason'

memes.label: Incorporeality
memes.description: 'The doctrine that God has no body — one of the two pillars of Maimonides'' theology alongside creation ex nihilo.'

memes.label: Intellect
memes.description: 'The faculty of intellectual cognition — central to Maimonides'' epistemology, prophecy theory, and doctrine of human perfection.'

memes.label: Isaac
memes.description: 'Biblical patriarch, son of Abraham, cited by Maimonides in his discussion of the uniqueness of Mosaic prophecy'

memes.label: Israel
memes.description: 'The people of Israel — the recipients of the Torah and the primary community addressed by Maimonides throughout the Guide.'

memes.label: Jacob
memes.description: 'The third patriarch of Israel, whose visions and the ladder narrative are cited by Maimonides in the Guide as examples of prophetic experience and whose descendants constitute the nation of Israel.'

memes.label: Job
memes.description: 'The biblical figure whose story of suffering and restoration Maimonides uses to illustrate divine providence and the problem of theodicy in the Guide.'

memes.label: John Philoponus
memes.description: 'Greek Christian philosopher and grammarian (6th–7th century CE) of Alexandria, whose critiques of Aristotle influenced the development of Islamic Kalām'

memes.label: Jonathan ben Uzziel
memes.description: 'The author of the Aramaic Targum to the Prophets, whose translation choices Maimonides frequently invokes as authoritative precedent for the non-literal interpretation of biblical anthropomorphisms.'

memes.label: Joseph ben Judah
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' disciple and the dedicatee of the Guide for the Perplexed — his questions about philosophy and Scripture prompted Maimonides to compose the work.'

memes.label: Kalam
memes.description: 'The school of Islamic scholastic theology (Kalâm) whose methods and doctrines Maimonides systematically critiques in the Guide for the Perplexed'

memes.label: Knowledge
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' epistemology — the pursuit of truth through reason, scripture, and their harmonious synthesis.'

memes.label: Law
memes.description: 'The divine Law (Torah) as Maimonides conceives it — a perfect legal-political-philosophical system that governs both body and soul, distinguishing Israel from all other nations.'

memes.label: Logic
memes.description: 'The science of valid reasoning that Maimonides identifies as the first of the preparatory sciences, essential training for anyone approaching metaphysics and theology'

memes.label: Maasé Beréschîth
memes.description: "In the Guide for the Perplexed, Maase Bereschith (the Account of the Beginning) is the esoteric biblical doctrine of creation, identified by Maimonides with Aristotelian physics and natural science."

memes.label: Maasé Mercabâ
memes.description: "In the Guide for the Perplexed, Maase Mercaba (the Account of the Chariot) is the esoteric biblical doctrine identified by Maimonides with Aristotelian metaphysics -- the science of God and the separate intelligences."

memes.label: Maimonides
memes.description: 'The author of the Guide for the Perplexed — rabbi, philosopher, physician, and codifier of Jewish law who synthesized Aristotelian philosophy with biblical revelation.'

memes.label: Matter and Form
memes.description: 'The Aristotelian doctrine of matter and form as the principles of all physical existence, adapted by Maimonides to monotheistic theology.'

memes.label: Miracles
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' theory that miracles were pre-programmed into the natural order at creation rather than representing ad hoc divine interventions'

memes.label: Moses
memes.description: 'The supreme prophet in Maimonides'' hierarchy — the only prophet whose prophecy was purely intellectual, without imaginative involvement.'

memes.label: Motécallemîn
memes.description: 'The Motécallemîn (Mutakallimūn) are the practitioners of Kalām, Islamic scholastic theology, whose methods and propositions Maimonides systematically analyzes and refutes in the Guide.'

memes.label: Necessary Existence
memes.description: 'The Avicennian metaphysical distinction — adopted by Maimonides — between that which exists necessarily through its own essence and that which is merely possible, requiring an external cause to exist'

memes.label: Onkelos
memes.description: 'The author of the standard Aramaic Targum of the Torah — Maimonides'' primary exegetical authority for the systematic elimination of anthropomorphism from Scripture.'

memes.label: Philo of Alexandria
memes.description: 'The Hellenistic Jewish philosopher whose allegorical method of interpreting Scripture established the intellectual tradition that Maimonides inherited and transformed in the Guide.'

memes.label: Plato
memes.description: 'The Greek philosopher whose doctrine of forms and cosmological account in the Timaeus are critically examined by Maimonides in the Guide.'

memes.label: Plotinus
memes.description: 'Greek Neoplatonic philosopher (3rd century CE), cited by Maimonides for his doctrine of negative theology and the ineffability of the divine cause'

memes.label: Prayer
memes.description: 'The act of addressing God through words — a practice that Maimonides subjects to philosophical analysis in the Guide, examining its purpose, its proper formulation, and the cognitive error of supposing that prayer changes God'

memes.label: Prophecy
memes.description: 'Maimonides'' theory of prophecy as the overflow of divine intellect through the active intellect to the perfected human faculties.'

memes.label: Prophets
memes.description: 'The class of individuals who receive divine revelation and whose prophetic experience Maimonides systematically analyzes — ranking its degrees, distinguishing true from false prophecy, and explaining its role in the economy of divine law'

memes.label: Providence
memes.description: 'Divine providence — God''s governance and care of the world, extending to individual beings according to their intellectual perfection.'

memes.label: Revelation
memes.description: 'The divine communication whereby God conveys knowledge to human beings — which Maimonides understands philosophically as an emanation of the Active Intellect rather than a literal descent or verbal event'

memes.label: Revelation at Sinai
memes.description: 'The event at Mount Sinai where God gave the Torah to Moses — the paradigmatic moment of divine revelation in Judaism.'

memes.label: Reward and Punishment
memes.description: 'The doctrine that God recompenses obedience and punishes transgression — which Maimonides reinterprets philosophically as the natural consequence of intellectual and moral perfection or deficiency rather than arbitrary divine decree'

memes.label: Saadia Gaon
memes.description: 'The first systematic Jewish philosopher and a key precursor to Maimonides in the development of Jewish Kalam.'

memes.label: Samuel
memes.description: 'The last of the Hebrew judges and first of the major prophets, invoked by Maimonides in the Guide as an example of the highest prophetic rank alongside Moses.'

memes.label: Solomon
memes.description: 'Biblical king and prophet, son of David, whose Proverbs and Ecclesiastes are cited by Maimonides as models of allegorical and wisdom literature'

memes.label: "Solomon ibn Gabirol"
memes.description: 'Eleventh-century Jewish Neoplatonic philosopher and poet whose "Fons Vitae" influenced medieval philosophy and is cited by Maimonides through the notes of Salomon Munk.'

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: 'The soul (néphesch) in Maimonides'' thought — encompassing vital, rational, and appetitive dimensions of human existence.'

memes.label: Sublunar World
memes.description: 'The sublunary realm of generation and corruption, distinguished from the eternal celestial spheres in Maimonides Aristotelian cosmology'

memes.label: Substance
memes.description: 'The Aristotelian category of substance (ousia) as discussed by Maimonides in relation to God, created beings, and the nature of existence'

memes.label: "Table des Chapitres"
memes.description: "Table of contents for Part I"

memes.label: Themistius
memes.description: 'The Aristotelian commentator whose epistemological principle — that being does not accommodate itself to opinions — Maimonides deploys as a decisive refutation of Kalam subjectivism.'

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: 'The concept of demonstrative or philosophical truth (vérité) as Maimonides understands it — knowledge established by rational proof, distinct from mere belief or tradition'

memes.label: Wisdom
memes.description: 'The intellectual perfection that Maimonides identifies as the highest human good — encompassing both theoretical wisdom (knowledge of God and the cosmos) and practical wisdom (the rational understanding of the commandments)'
